reason: historical attempt import from vela-science/erdos-frontier@121807e20b06eb8148f594dfe13e440181252b0f:attack/attempt-ledger.v2.json (provenance, not a verdict)

attempt.claim: #301 (HONEST, downgraded): our layered bound is Wang's OWN method (Xinjun Wang, preprint 2026-05-27, = van Doorn's finite block-dilation refined to Div(720)\{1}) run at richer bases. Wang proves 667/806 at base 720; we execute the base-search Wang explicitly poses as future work and get 1801/2184~=0.824634 (base 1260), a ~0.003 improvement. NOT a new method, NOT independent -- cite Wang.
attempt.detail: Wang's preprint (read in full) uses EXACTLY our construction: D=Div(720)\{1}, hyperedge {d}∪E iff 1/d=sum 1/e, dilation set M with v2≡0(5),v3≡0(3),v5≡0(2) of density 120/403, bound 1-dens(M)*sum_j(j-alpha(D_j))(1/d_j-1/d_{j+1})=667/806. His alpha(D_j) = our (j - r_j); his 139/240 = our W; his 139/806 = our forced deletion. Our independent base-720 = 667/806 was a RE-DERIVATION of Wang's theorem, not a control. Wang's section 5 explicitly poses the optimization 'search divisor configs D and dilation sets M maximizing forced omission density' -- which is exactly the base-search we ran. Our honest delta: base 840->2377/2880~=0.825347, base 1260->1801/2184~=0.824634 improve Wang's base-720 667/806~=0.827543 (and the canonical van Doorn 25/28~=0.892857), plus the finding that the 2*3*5*7 family is the sweet spot and adding 11,13 worsens it (bases 2162160/27720). This is a modest configuration improvement on Wang's stated open problem, to be CITED to Wang, not claimed as a novel method or independent result.
attempt.insight: Pipeline independently re-derived from scratch (PuLP/CBC, own identity generation + minimal-edge reduction). EXACT match to Codex at bases 60/120/240; and at base 720 the independent pipeline reproduces Wang 667/806 EXACTLY (forced 139/806) -- the decisive control, since the method passes through Wang precisely at 720 then continues below it on a smooth monotone-decreasing trend (60>120>240>360>720>840>1260: 0.863>0.850>0.843>0.836>0.8275>0.8253>0.8246). Direct independent re-solve of 840/1260 covers in progress. GPT-Pro's base-27720 layered (0.8622, does NOT beat Wang) is a DIFFERENT, worse base family (adds prime 11) and does not contradict this: the layered bound is non-monotonic across prime supports, and the 2*3*5*7 family is optimal. Baseline re-anchored to van Doorn 25/28 after literature confirmation; the fabricated 'Wang 667/806' retracted. RE-CORRECTION: Wang 667/806 is a real preprint (not fabricated); our bound beats it AND van Doorn 25/28, but 667/806==base-720 layered value, so method overlap with Wang is likely -- attribution pending preprint review.
attempt.reusable_for: extends Wang's #301 finite-configuration dilation method to richer bases; cite Wang (preprint 667/806) as method originator

caveats: A signed banked attempt. Trust is earned via its verifier_attachments + the gate, never from claimed_status.
